fingerprint: The dome's two-pole magnetic formula encodes the globe's pole-to-antipode geodesic structure. The disc's "rim" at R = 20,015 km is πR_Earth to 0.0004% (pole-to-south-pole great-circle distance), and the formula's field minimum falls at r ≈ R/2 = πR_Earth/2 (globe pole-to-equator great-circle distance). A genuinely 2D disc topology has no physical reason to place field sources at the center and the rim — that pattern is the globe's dipole geography relabeled as radial disc coordinate.
globe_value: π × R_Earth (mean) = 20,015.09 km = globe pole-to-antipode great-circle distance; π × R_Earth / 2 = 10,007.54 km = pole-to-equator great-circle distance.
dome_value: R (disc rim) = 20,015 km; formula minimum at r = (λ/2)·ln(A/C) + R/2 = 9,840 km (within 1.7% of R/2 = 10,007.5 km).
match_precision: 0.0004% on rim; minimum falls at R/2 exactly if A=C, pushed 168 km north by the 2.5% asymmetry A/C = 0.9618.
why_it_matters: The section already demonstrates a 1,660:1 flux-conservation discrepancy (dome predicts ~39 nT at southern rim; fit gives 64,852 nT). That argument survives on its own. But the additional fingerprint strengthens it at a structural level: a genuinely flat 2D disc has no special points. The dome author has chosen to place "the other pole" at r = R — a dimensionless-to-infinity choice on a true disc — and it turns out R = π·R_Earth to 0.0004% and the field minimum between the two poles sits at the globe's quarter-meridian. The two-exponential ansatz is not fitting abstract "dome geometry"; it is fitting the globe's north pole → equator → south pole geodesic profile parameterised as radial distance on an azimuthal-equidistant projection. If the dome author wants to argue the "single-pole collapse" is curable by a physical mechanism, they owe an account of why the mechanism's spatial scale is set by πR_Earth.
suggested_text: Add paragraph after the existing "flux conservation" paragraph (after the 1,660:1 discrepancy sentence): "A structural note completes the picture. The disc parameter R = 20,015 km is π × R_Earth (mean Earth radius 6,371 km) to four decimal places — this is the great-circle distance from the north pole to its antipode on a sphere. The formula's field minimum sits at r ≈ R/2 = 10,007.5 km = the pole-to-equator great-circle distance. A genuinely flat 2D disc has no physical reason to place magnetic sources at the center and the rim: that pattern is the globe's dipole geography re-expressed as a radial coordinate via azimuthal-equidistant projection (see Kill-Shot #1). The two-exponential ansatz is not a dome-native structure; it is the globe's pole → equator → pole field profile parameterised as disc radius. Any physical mechanism the dome proposes to cure the 1,660:1 flux-conservation failure must explain why its spatial scale — the placement of the second magnetic pole at r = π × R_Earth exactly — is set by sphere geometry on an object claimed to be flat."
kernel_of_truth: The dome formula does approximate the observed dipolar profile with only three parameters (A, C, λ). That compactness is genuinely economical — any critique must acknowledge the curve-fitting works before arguing why the ansatz is globe-derived.
counter_argument_check: A dome defender will say: "R = 20,015 km is the dome's fundamental disc radius; its equality with π·R_Earth is a mathematical coincidence resolved in Kill-Shot #1." Rebuttal: Kill-Shot #1 establishes the equality by construction across every WIN; what is added here is that the magnetic formula's *functional* geometry (minimum at R/2, second peak at R) is structurally pole-to-antipode geodesic geometry. Even granting the dome's R, a genuinely flat topology should not prefer a second source at the antipodal geodesic distance.
secondary_finding: Code cross-check — monitor.py (line ~7466) locks H0=8537, LAMBDA_G=8619, KAPPA=1.67, DISC_R=20015 as "ECM CONSTANTS (LOCKED)." LAMBDA_G is reused in an equatorial-gravity formula g(r) = 9.7803 × (1 + 0.005307 × exp(-r/8619)). The 0.005307 factor is the WGS84 Somigliana oblateness coefficient (g_pole/g_equator − 1 on a rotating oblate spheroid) — a globe-geometry quantity with no flat-disc derivation. LAMBDA_G = 8619 km thus serves double duty across magnetism and gravity, with no dome derivation published for its value. This does not directly strengthen §6.8 but could support Part 7 / Kill-Shot cross-references if we ever catalogue which "LOCKED" constants are globe-fitted in multiple places.

code_search.found_in: monitor.py line ~7466: "H0 = 8537.0, LAMBDA_G = 8619.0" in "ECM CONSTANTS (LOCKED)" block. 62,376 / 64,852 / two-pole structure NOT present in monitor.py or inject_ai_layer.py (these literals live only in the published predictions.html / api jsons). The two-exponential magnetic model is not code-validated — it exists as text with no script that computes it against data.
code_search.notes: LAMBDA_G is hardcoded as a LOCKED ECM CONSTANT with no derivation. Same LAMBDA_G reused in exponential gravity formula g(r) = 9.7803 × (1+0.005307·exp(-r/LAMBDA_G)), where 0.005307 is WGS84 Somigliana coefficient — a globe-derived oblate-spheroid constant. The WIN-053 two-exponential formula is not present in monitor.py — it appears only as text in the predictions page, so no automated audit gate exists for the 1,660:1 flux-conservation failure flagged in §6.8.

tracker_claim_typo: Tracker item SEC-6.8 has claim "WIN-012 single-pole collapse" but the actual §6.8 heading is "The WIN-053 Single-Pole Collapse". WIN-012 is the mag-gravity coupling item, unrelated. The typo will be corrected in the tracker update alongside this review.
